steps.title: Find the first line
steps.content:
For $x+y=53$, set one variable equal to $0$ at a time:
$$
(0,53)\quad\text{and}\quad(53,0)
$$
So this line slopes downward through those intercepts.

steps.title: Find the second line
steps.content:
For $11x+18y=730$, set $x=0$ to get
$$
y=\frac{730}{18}=40.5
$$
Set $y=0$ to get
$$
x=\frac{730}{11}=66.4
$$
These are about $(0,41)$ and $(66,0)$. This line also slopes downward.

steps.title: Match the graph
steps.content: The matching graph must show one downward line through $(0,53)$ and $(53,0)$ and another downward line through about $(0,41)$ and $(66,0)$. The answer is B.
